title: Container not scrolling due to overflow settings
symptom: The container does not scroll despite attempts to set fixed heights and overflow properties. The content remains static within the parent container.
applies_when: Container is set to a fixed height
Overflow properties are not correctly configured
not_this_if: Container is intentionally set to not scroll
Parent container has no height restrictions
root_cause: The scrolling issue arises when the parent container does not have the overflow property set to 'scroll' or 'auto', and the child container does not exceed the parent's dimensions. Without proper overflow settings, the content cannot scroll.
fix_steps: Select the parent div and set its overflow property to 'scroll' or 'auto'.
Ensure the child div has content that exceeds the height of the parent div.
agent_instruction: Set the parent div's overflow property to 'scroll' and ensure the child div's content overflows.
